headline: Oil Prices Fall on Fresh Hopes for a Swift End to Iran War
summary: The retreat follows a a 51% surge in crude oil prices in March for their largest monthly gain in nearly six years.

headline: Viper Energy, Inc., a Subsidiary of Diamondback Energy, Inc., Schedules First Quarter 2026 Conference Call for May 5, 2026
summary: MIDLAND, Texas, April 01, 2026 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- Viper Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: VNOM) (“Viper”), a subsidiary of Diamondback Energy, Inc. (NASDAQ: FANG) (“Diamondback”), today announced that it plans to release first quarter 2026 financial results on May 4, 2026 after the market closes. In connection with the earnings release, Viper will host a conference call and webcast for investors and analysts to discuss its results for the first quarter of 2026 on Tuesday, May 5, 2026 at 10:00 a.m. CT. headline: Trump Says Iran Has Asked for a Cease-Fire. Oil Prices Are Falling.
summary: Oil prices were sliding on Wednesday after President Donald Trump said that Iran’s President had asked for a cease-fire, boosting investors’ hopes that the war in the Middle East could soon end.  Brent  crude international futures were down 1.8% at $102.06 a barrel in early trading, after falling below the $100 a barrel level earlier in the day for the first time since Friday.  West Texas Intermediate  U.S. futures were down 1.2% at $100.25 a barrel.

headline: Exxon, Chevron, and More Oil Stocks Fall. Why That Could Be the New Normal.
summary: Investors may have to get used to selloffs in oil stocks, given that the market is betting that the Iran war could end soon.

headline: Oil Back Above $100 a Barrel After Brief Dip. Trump Renews NATO Threat.
summary: Oil prices climbed back above $100 a barrel Wednesday, after briefly falling below that key level as President Donald Trump gave investors hope that the war in the Middle East could soon end.  Brent  crude international futures were down 1% at $102.95 a barrel in early trading, after falling below the $100 a barrel level earlier in the day for the first time since Friday.  West Texas Intermediate  U.S. futures were down 1.2% lower at $100.21 a barrel.

Brent  crude international futures were down 1% at $102.95 a barrel in early trading, after falling below the $100 a barrel level earlier in the day for the first time since Friday.  West Texas Intermediate  U.S. futures were down 1.2% lower at $100.21 a barrel.

headline: Oil Drops Below $100 a Barrel Ahead of Trump Addressing the Nation About Iran War
summary: Oil prices were dropping on Wednesday after President Donald Trump gave investors hope that the war in the Middle East could soon end.  Brent  international futures slid 4.3% to $99.63 a barrel in early trading, falling below the $100 a barrel level for the first time since Friday.  West Texas Intermediate  U.S. futures were 4.6% lower at $96.77 a barrel.
